title: Philibertia suberecta

The plant is native to the Americas and is often found in dry regions. Philibertia suberecta is classified as a subshrub or a shrubby climber, with a unique growth habit. Its classification is further supported by its morphological characteristics and genetic analysis.

The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in wound healing, pain relief, and the management of various health conditions.
